mnemonics: PIT (Pthreads, Implicit, Thread) - Pthreads is a common library that Implicitly manages Threads.
keyPoints: Thread libraries provide an API for creating and managing threads.
The three main types are: Pthreads (POSIX, common on Unix/Linux), Win32 threads (for Windows), and language-specific libraries like Java threads.
Libraries can be implemented at the user level or the kernel level, which affects performance and functionality.
commonMistakes: Assuming all thread libraries (e.g., Pthreads, Win32) work identically across different operating systems.
Confusing user-level threads (managed by a library) with kernel-level threads (managed by the OS).
